text:
- Mo checks Form C to find out what documents she has to prepare before the first appointment. Form C tells her she must send the following to the court (and send copies to Pat).

- A financial statement (Form E) - the form you fill in to tell the court about your finances. Buy our extended guide for more information about this form and how to complete it.
- ES1 summary - this is the form for the judge which summarises the key details of the case. You and your ex need to do your very best to agree what goes in this summary.
- ES2 schedule - this is a table or schedule of all your assets (things of value) and income and any debts.
- A valuation of the family home from an estate agent (agreed with Pat if possible).
- Three sets of information about possible properties that would meet her housing needs and another three sets of information about possible properties that would meet Pat’s needs.
- Information about her borrowing capacity from a mortgage adviser (who Mo and Pat jointly ask to provide, if possible).
- A questionnaire - a list of any other information or documents you want your ex to provide. Find more information about how to prepare a questionnaire in our extended guide .
- A position statement - this is a short statement that sets out your position on the case and what you would like the court to order.
- Form FM5 - this is the form you need to fill in to tell the court what efforts you have made to resolve your dispute before going to court. You do not have to fill this in if you have claimed an exemption from going to a MIAM due to domestic abuse in your relationship.
- A completed Form G - the form that asks you to tell the court and your ex whether or not you will be ready to negotiate an agreement at the first appointment.
- Form H - this is the form you need to fill in to tell the court what you have spent on the case so far.

- Mo checks Form C again and makes a note of the different deadlines – the dates when she must get these documents to the court and Pat.

- Mo sends a copy of her Form A and Form C to the Tunstone Building Society – the company that lent her and Pat the money to buy their home.

- Over several weeks, Mo collects together all the information she needs to fill in Form E fully.

- Mo also starts collecting the documents she must attach to Form E as evidence of what she says in the form.

metadata.source: How to apply for a financial order without a lawyer: Advicenow
